meaning: n.古希腊的银币名,古希腊的重量单位
examples: The drachma disappeared, replaced by the euro when Greece joined the single European currency.
The new drachma would plummet, making Greece’s debt burden even more onerous.
The word "dram" translates into English as "money", and is cognate with the Greek drachma.
Germany could go back to the deutschmark, but Greece cannot go back to the drachma.
To generate confidence in the drachma in the midst of a crisis would be very challenging.
